preamble: The transformation of the high front rounded vowel [y] into the mid back rounded vowel [o] is a form of backing and lowering, typically observed in historical sound shifts or as a result of phonetic drift in speakers of languages without front rounded vowels.

examples.note: Resolving complex or rounded vowels into simpler back rounded categories during the transition from Middle to Modern Indo-Aryan dialects.
Documented phonetic drift where L2 learners replace unfamiliar front rounded vowels with acoustically similar back rounded vowels.

sources: Phonetic research on L2 vowel acquisition and drift.
